questions.question: Explain the algorithm to find the middle element of a linked list in a single traversal. What are the positions returned for odd and even length lists?
keyPoints: Use two pointers - slow and fast
Slow moves one step, fast moves two steps
When fast reaches null, slow is at middle
Even length: two middle nodes (second returned)

questions.question: Describe the process to detect a cycle in a linked list using Floyd's algorithm. What is the time and space complexity?
keyPoints: Slow moves one node, fast moves two

questions.question: How do you find the Nth node from the end of a linked list? Explain with an example where N=3.
keyPoints: Move first pointer N positions ahead
When first reaches end, second points to Nth from end

questions.question: Write an algorithm to remove duplicates from a sorted linked list. What is the time complexity?
keyPoints: Traverse with current and previous pointers
If current.data equals previous.data, skip current

questions.question: Explain how to check whether a linked list is a palindrome. What auxiliary space is required?
keyPoints: Compare first half with reversed second half
For odd length, skip middle element

questions.question: Describe the procedure to merge two sorted linked lists into a single sorted list.
How do you rotate a linked list by k positions to the right? Explain with an example.
keyPoints: Handle k > n by k = k % n
Make list circular by connecting tail to head
Find new head at position n-k

questions.question: Explain the algorithm to reverse a singly linked list. Write pseudocode and analyze its time and space complexity. Also explain how you would reverse it in groups of k nodes.
keyPoints: Iterative reversal using three pointers (prev, curr, next)
Reverse each node pointer to previous node
Update head to prev at end
For k-group reversal: reverse first k nodes, then recursively reverse remaining
Handle cases where remaining nodes are less than k

questions.question: Write an algorithm to find the intersection point of two linked lists. Explain all steps including handling lists of different lengths. What is the time and space complexity?
keyPoints: When addresses match, that's intersection point

questions.question: Explain how to add two numbers represented as linked lists where each node contains a single digit. The digits are stored in reverse order (least significant digit at head). Write algorithm and complexity analysis.
keyPoints: Traverse both simultaneously adding digits with carry
Create new node for each sum digit

questions.question: Describe the algorithm to pairwise swap adjacent nodes in a linked list without swapping data. Write pseudocode and explain all pointer adjustments needed.
keyPoints: For each pair: adjust prev.next, first.next, second.next, next.prev
Update prev to first after swap
Continue until less than 2 nodes remain
Need to track four pointers per iteration
